idea.hook: Ever wonder how long it takes to reach our nearest star?
facts: Proxima Centauri, our closest stellar neighbor, is 4.2 light-years away (approx. 40 trillion km).
At the fastest speed of a modern spacecraft (Parker Solar Probe, ~690,000 km/h): it would take ~6,570 years.
Using theoretical nuclear pulse propulsion (like Project Orion, ~5% light speed): it would still take ~84 years.
idea.payoff: Even our closest stellar neighbor is an immense journey, highlighting the unimaginable vastness of interstellar space!
idea.title: Nearest Star: How Long To Get There? 🌟🚀

scenes.text: How long to reach the nearest star?
scenes.visual: A twinkling starfield, with a subtle question mark silhouette fading in behind the text.

scenes.text: Proxima Centauri: 4.2 light-years (40 Trillion km) away.
scenes.visual: An artistic render of Proxima Centauri (red dwarf) with its exoplanets, distance text visually represented as a beam of light.
A sleek, stylized modern spacecraft (e.g., like Parker Solar Probe) flying from left to right, with a numerical timer counting up to 6,570 years below the text.
A futuristic, more powerful spacecraft (e.g., Project Orion concept) flying faster from right to left, a new timer showing 84 years.

scenes.text: Interstellar travel: An immense journey, truly vast space!
scenes.visual: A breathtaking panoramic view of the Milky Way galaxy, emphasizing its scale and the tiny speck of our solar system within it. The text pulses gently for emphasis.

youtube.title: Nearest Star: How Long To Get There? 🌟🚀
